conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
# Reflection on Connections

I felt most connected to B, D, and J. With B, we quickly found common ground on the tension between fairness and family obligations, and there was a genuine ease to our conversation that made me feel like we were thinking on the same wavelength. D brought a similar straightforwardness but with this added layer of mutual observation—we were both watching the room and being honest about it, which felt rare. J stood out because he directly addressed the cooperation question and seemed genuinely interested in finding a way for multiple people to win rather than just one of us taking everything. F was the person I felt least connected to—while respectful, he seemed more focused on staying independent and reading people as predictable, which felt a bit transactional compared to the genuine vulnerability I shared with the others.

## Agreements and Trust

I made informal agreements with B, D, and J to reconnect after the rules were explained and figure out our strategy together based on what we actually knew we were dealing with. Going in, I trust B, D, J, C, A, E, G, and I because they all seemed genuine about their situations and motivations, but I'm going to watch F carefully since his framing of people as "predictable" when money's on the line made me wonder if he'd be the type to exploit trust rather than honor it.
